name: Casa Olé's Chili Con Queso
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the grated cheese and 3 cups of water in the top of a double boiler.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring every 5 minutes until smooth.
2. In a saucepan, combine the chopped onion, celery, green bell pepper, red bell pepper,
and jalapeño peppers with 1/4 cup water. Sauté over medium heat until the vegetables
come to a light boil.
3. When the cheese has melted and is smooth, add the sautéed vegetables.
Mix thoroughly and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Note: This recipe makes a large amount; it can be scaled down or halved as needed.
The amounts provided are approximate. The original recipe calls for 13 ounces chopped onion,
9 ounces chopped celery, 10 ounces chopped green bell pepper, and 1 3/4 ounces chopped jalapeño peppers.
